profile.name: Srikanth N
profile.bio: Aspiring SOC Analyst | Cybersecurity Engineering Student | SIEM | Ethical Hacking | VAPT| Incident Response Threat Detection | Cloud Security
profile.aboutBio:
Pre-Final Year B.E. Computer Science & Engineering (Cyber Security) student pursuing an (Honours degree), with a strong
foundation in network security, digital forensics, ethical hacking, and security operations. Completed four internships across
cybercrime agencies and technology organizations, gaining hands-on experience in forensic investigations, fund trail analysis,
cybercrime case support, and secure application development. Skilled in SIEM monitoring, threat detection, log analysis,
incident response, vulnerability assessment, and security analysis. Passionate about cybersecurity, cloud security, and AIdriven security automation, with a keen interest in SOC operations, threat hunting, and cyber defence. Seeking opportunities to
apply technical and analytical skills in real-world security environments.

hackathons.title: IBM Naan Mudhalvan EBPL Hackathon
hackathons.desc:
The Connected Home Security System is an innovative IoT-based solution designed to provide
homeowners with smart, real-time protection. The system integrates motion sensors, cameras,
and microcontrollers to detect unusual activity, send alerts, and allow remote monitoring through
a mobile app. By using modern IoT technology, cloud connectivity, and AI-based surveillance,
this project aims to make home security smart, affordable, and accessible for everyone. Users
can view live footage, receive instant notifications, and control devices remotely, ensuring
round-the-clock safety

blogs.title: SQL Injection Bypass Technique via Filter Evasion
blogs.excerpt: Detailing how web application filters can be bypassed using hex encoding and character set manipulation in database payloads.
blogs.content:
### SQL Injection Filter Evasion

During a recent VAPT exercise, I encountered an input filter that stripped standard SQL keywords like `SELECT`, `UNION`, and `WHERE` case-insensitively.

#### The Filter Bypass
By using SQL comment inline injection and alternative encodings, we can construct payloads that the regex filter misses but the SQL parser reconstructs:

```sql
-- Standard payload (Blocked)
UNION SELECT username, password FROM users;

-- Evaded payload (Bypassed)
UNI/**/ON SEL/**/ECT username, password FR/**/OM users;
```

Additionally, utilizing hex-encoding for strings allowed bypass of single-quote filters:
`0x61646d696e` instead of `'admin'`.

#### Prevention
Use parameterized queries (Prepared Statements) rather than trying to sanitize strings via regular expression filters.

blogs.title: Privilege Escalation on Linux: SUID Binary Abuse
blogs.excerpt: A walk-through of abusing misconfigured SUID permissions on standard utilities to obtain root privileges on target Linux machines.
blogs.content:
### Linux SUID Privilege Escalation

SUID (Set owner User ID on execution) is a special file permission in Linux that allows users to run a binary with the permissions of the file owner (often root).

If administrators grant SUID privileges to commands that can read files or run system binaries, attackers can exploit them.

#### The Discovery
First, we scan for SUID files:
```bash
find / -perm -4000 -type f 2>/dev/null
```

Suppose `/usr/bin/find` has the SUID bit set. According to [GTFOBins](https://gtfobins.github.io), we can execute commands through it:

```bash
find . -exec /bin/sh -p \; -quit
```

This executes `/bin/sh` with inherited root privileges, giving us a root shell!

#### Mitigation
Review SUID bits and restrict them strictly to binary files that require them. Never set SUID on scripting interpreters or editors.
